name: ArangoDB
description: Native multi-model database supporting document, graph, and key-value data models with AQL query language

license.comment: Changed from Apache-2.0 to BSL-1.1 starting with v3.12. Community Edition binaries have additional 100GB limit. Converts to Apache-2.0 after 4 years.

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted via Community/Enterprise editions. ArangoGraph is the official managed service. Available on AWS Marketplace.

initial-release.comment: First released in spring 2012 as AvocadoDB, renamed to ArangoDB in May 2012. First commit October 2011.

primary-language.comment: C++ (50.5%), with significant JavaScript (45.2%) for Foxx microservices and tooling

data-model.comment: Native multi-model: document, graph, and key-value in a single engine. Also includes full-text search via ArangoSearch.

topology.comment: Cluster uses Coordinators (stateless routers), DB-Servers (data shards), and Agents (Raft consensus for metadata). Each shard has a leader with followers.

sharding.comment: Default hash-based sharding on document key. SmartGraphs and SatelliteGraphs (Enterprise) allow optimized data placement.

replication-factor.comment: Replication factor configurable per collection, typically 2-3 for production

multi-region.comment: Supports both active-passive and active-active multi-region deployments. Recommends async replication for cross-region writes.

cap-position.comment: CP system - prefers consistency over availability during network partitions. Uses Raft for agent consensus.

consistency-model.comment: Provides Causal-Consistency for transactions. Local Snapshot Isolation per DB-Server. Full ACID on single server or OneShard deployments.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID on single server and OneShard. In multi-shard clusters, distributed transactions may not be fully atomic if a node fails during commit.

transaction-scope.comment: Single-partition is fully ACID. Cross-partition transactions supported but atomicity during commit may be compromised if DB-Server fails.

isolation-levels.comment: Local Snapshot Isolation - all operations in a transaction see the same snapshot of data on each DB-Server.

distributed-transactions.comment: Introduced in v3.5. Supports Stream Transactions API for multi-document transactions with explicit begin/commit/abort.

query-language.comment: AQL (ArangoDB Query Language) - SQL-like declarative language for documents, graphs, and key-value operations

sql-compatibility.comment: AQL is SQL-like but not SQL-compatible. Different syntax for JOINs (graph traversals), no standard SQL wire protocol.

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports persistent indexes, TTL indexes, geo indexes, fulltext indexes (deprecated in favor of ArangoSearch), and inverted indexes

joins.comment: Distributed joins introduced in v3.5. Graph traversals and document joins across shards. SmartJoins (Enterprise) optimize co-located data.

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support in AQL: COLLECT (GROUP BY), AGGREGATE, WITH COUNT, etc.

full-text-search.comment: ArangoSearch: integrated C++ full-text search engine with BM25/TFIDF ranking, phrase search, fuzzy matching, 30 languages supported

max-cluster-size.comment: Benchmarked up to 80 nodes (640 vCPUs) on Mesosphere DCOS. Scales linearly for document operations.

write-throughput.comment: ~1GB/sec sustained write load. ~1,730 docs/sec per vCPU. Measured on Mesosphere DCOS cluster.

read-latency-p99.comment: Median latency < 5ms, 95th percentile < 30ms in cluster benchmark under write load

auto-scaling.comment: Manual scaling by adding/removing DB-Servers. ArangoGraph managed service may offer more automated scaling.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Requires manual resharding or SmartGraphs/SatelliteGraphs (Enterprise) for optimized data distribution

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades via Kubernetes operator. Sequential upgrade of servers one at a time. Recommended to take backup before upgrading.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-free document model allows flexible schema evolution. Index creation is online.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: PITR via Hot Backups (Enterprise Edition only, introduced in v3.5.1). Creates consistent snapshots with minimal operational impact.

backup-strategy.comment: Hot Backups (Enterprise): near-instantaneous snapshots using hard links. Also supports arangodump logical backups and physical (cold) backups.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official kube-arangodb operator. Production ready, supports K8s 1.18+. Provides PersistentVolumes, DC-to-DC replication.

go-driver.comment: Official go-driver maintained by ArangoDB. Version 2 is the current recommended version.

java-driver.comment: Official Java driver. Spring Data ArangoDB integration available for Spring-based applications.

python-driver.comment: python-arango is the official Python driver

c-cpp-driver.comment: No dedicated C/C++ driver; the database itself is written in C++ and uses HTTP/VelocyPack API

orm-support.comment: Spring Data ArangoDB for Java. No support for traditional SQL ORMs due to AQL query language.

open-source.comment: Changed to BSL-1.1 (source-available, not OSI-approved) from v3.12. Converts to Apache-2.0 after 4 years. Drivers remain Apache-2.0.

free-tier.comment: 14-day free trial on ArangoGraph. Community Edition free for local/non-commercial use with 100GB data limit.

pricing-model.comment: ArangoGraph pricing based on cloud provider, node count, memory, and storage. Starts at $0.20/hour.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Estimate based on $0.20/hour base pricing. Actual cost depends on cloud provider, region, and configuration.

change-data-capture.comment: No native built-in CDC. Can use replication API or WAL tailing with external tools. Kafka Connect sink available via community.

watch-subscribe.comment: No native live queries or change feeds. Long-standing feature request (Issue #602). Requires polling or external solutions.

triggers.comment: No database-side triggers. Foxx microservices can implement application-level logic but not true database triggers.

event-streaming.comment: Community Kafka Connect sink connector available. No official vendor-maintained streaming integrations.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count. ArangoDB tag exists on Stack Overflow.
